behaviors.description: Products marked as out-of-stock are excluded from recommendation results regardless of relevance score
behaviors.justification: Real failures occur when availability checking is skipped, incorrectly applied, or product availability data is malformed. A unit test with mocked availability could pass while real data (missing fields, null values, stale cache) causes filtering to fail. Integration testing with realistic product data catches these data-boundary issues.
behaviors.test_description: Load product catalog with mixed availability states; generate recommendations for users; verify no out-of-stock products appear in results regardless of their relevance scores or ranking weights
behaviors.plan_consistent_note: Test requires wiring product catalog, availability state, and filtering logic together—pure unit mocking of availability status would miss real failures in data retrieval or state handling.

behaviors.description: User preference filter correctly excludes product categories user has explicitly blocked in settings
behaviors.justification: Real failures occur when user settings are missing, incorrectly formatted, or category names don't match between settings and product catalog. Mock settings with perfect data would pass; real settings failures stay hidden. Integration testing with actual user preference data catches schema mismatches and data state issues.
behaviors.test_description: Create users with various blocked category preferences in settings; load product catalog; generate recommendations; verify blocked categories are completely absent from results
behaviors.plan_consistent_note: Test must wire user settings retrieval, category matching, and product filtering together. Unit mocking of blocked categories would miss real failures in settings retrieval or category name normalization.

behaviors.description: Recommendation algorithm ranks products matching user's recent browsing patterns higher than products with no browsing signal
behaviors.justification: Real failures occur when browsing history is sparse, stale, or missing temporal context—edge cases a fixture can't reproduce. Algorithm ranking bugs (incorrect weighting, missing category match, timestamp handling) emerge when processing real browsing data. Integration testing with realistic historical patterns catches these algorithm correctness issues.
behaviors.test_description: Create user with browsing history concentrated in specific product categories (e.g., electronics); generate recommendations; verify products from browsed categories rank higher than products from completely unbrowsed categories
behaviors.plan_consistent_note: Test requires real browsing history, product catalog, and ranking algorithm all wired together. Unit tests with perfect fixtures could hide algorithm bugs in how it processes temporal browsing data.

behaviors.description: Pagination returns correct page of results with stable ordering—requesting same page twice returns identical results if underlying data hasn't changed
behaviors.justification: Real failures occur when database ordering is non-deterministic (hash-based ordering, unindexed queries, concurrent updates), or pagination offset/limit math fails at real data scales. Small mock datasets accidentally have stable ordering; real databases don't. Integration testing with actual data storage catches non-deterministic ordering and pagination boundary bugs.
behaviors.test_description: Generate recommendations for user; request page 2 twice without intervening state changes; assert both requests return identical ranked product lists with identical pagination metadata
behaviors.plan_consistent_note: Test requires real data storage and actual query execution. Unit tests with 20-item mock datasets would always return stable ordering, missing real non-determinism in database result sets.

behaviors.description: Recommendation algorithm surfaces at least one product from a category user hasn't browsed before (serendipity requirement), avoiding filter bubble
behaviors.justification: Real failures occur when serendipity selection fails to find unbrowsed categories (catalog mismatch, browsing history incomplete, category name normalization fails). Mocked selections can hardcode serendipity while real algorithm gets stuck recommending only familiar categories. Integration testing with real catalog and browsing history verifies the selection logic actually finds diverse options.
behaviors.test_description: Create user with browsing history concentrated in 2-3 categories; load full product catalog; generate recommendations; verify at least one product from a category user hasn't browsed appears in top results
behaviors.plan_consistent_note: Test requires real product catalog, user browsing history, and serendipity selection logic. Unit mocks could always return a serendipity product while real selection logic fails.

behaviors.description: New user with no purchase or browsing history receives recommendations based on overall popularity rather than empty result set
behaviors.justification: Real failures occur when the fallback doesn't trigger correctly, popularity calculation is broken, or popularity rankings are missing/invalid. Unit tests with mocked popularity scores would pass; real popularity data failures (empty rankings, missing products, stale scores) stay hidden. Integration testing with actual popularity data catches fallback logic and ranking bugs.
behaviors.test_description: Create new user account with zero purchase and browsing history; generate recommendations; assert recommendations are non-empty and products are ordered by overall popularity metrics
behaviors.plan_consistent_note: Test requires new user creation, empty state verification, and real popularity calculation. Unit mocking of popularity rankings would miss bugs in the calculation or fallback trigger condition.

behaviors.description: Click tracking records correct product ID, position in list, and timestamp for each recommendation user interacts with
behaviors.justification: Real failures occur when database writes fail, timestamps are invalid/out-of-order, position calculation is off, or data corruption occurs on storage. Mock writes always succeed and data always persists; real database failures (constraint violations, write errors, stale reads) only appear with actual persistence. Integration testing with real database catches data recording and integrity bugs.
behaviors.test_description: Generate recommendations for user; simulate multiple clicks at specific positions in the ranked list; query recorded click events; verify product IDs, positions, and timestamps match expectations with strict ordering guarantees
behaviors.plan_consistent_note: Test requires actual database writes and reads. Mock storage would never fail, hiding real persistence bugs, constraint violations, or timestamp generation issues.

behaviors.description: Response time for generating recommendations stays under 200ms for a catalog of 100,000 products
behaviors.justification: Real performance failures only appear under realistic load and scale. Unit tests with small datasets (10-100 items) naturally complete in <200ms, hiding algorithmic slowness, unindexed queries, and N+1 problems. System testing with actual 100k product catalog and realistic infrastructure exposes true performance characteristics and bottlenecks.
behaviors.test_description: Load 100,000 product catalog into production-equivalent database; generate recommendations for typical user profile; measure end-to-end response time; assert completion under 200ms; test across multiple user profiles (new user, power user, various category preferences)
behaviors.plan_consistent_note: Test absolutely requires real scale. Small integration tests cannot expose performance degradation that only manifests at 100k products due to query complexity, memory usage, or algorithm scaling.

behaviors.description: When recommended product is purchased by user, recommendation algorithm deprioritizes it in subsequent requests
behaviors.justification: Real failures occur when purchase state isn't properly retrieved, applied to ranking, or propagated to subsequent requests. Mock purchase state with perfect data would pass; real purchase data issues (stale cache, state retrieval failure, ranking logic bug) hide until integration testing. Integration testing with actual purchase tracking verifies state handling and ranking application.
behaviors.test_description: Create user with purchase history; generate recommendations; simulate purchasing a recommended product; generate new recommendations; verify previously purchased product is ranked lower than comparable unpurchased products
behaviors.plan_consistent_note: Test requires real purchase tracking, state retrieval, and ranking logic. Unit tests with mocked purchase state would miss bugs in state propagation or ranking algorithm application.

behaviors.description: Recommendation results are deterministic for the same user state—requesting recommendations twice with no intervening activity returns identical ranked list
behaviors.justification: Real non-determinism emerges from database ordering (unindexed queries, hash randomization), algorithm implementation (floating-point precision, unordered collections), or concurrent updates. Small fixtures accidentally have deterministic ordering; real data storage and algorithms don't. Integration testing with actual database and repeated execution catches non-deterministic behavior in real systems.
behaviors.test_description: Generate recommendations for user; request same recommendations again 100x without intervening state changes; verify all 100 responses return identical ranked product lists with identical ordering
behaviors.plan_consistent_note: Test requires repeated execution against real data storage. Unit tests with small mocked datasets would naturally return identical results, hiding real non-determinism in query ordering or algorithm implementation.
